notes: SECURITY (7/10): Strong fundamentals — timingSafeEqual for HMAC+password, HMAC-signed session cookies, HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite=Strict, CSRF via custom header, anti-enumeration on login (dummy hash) and password reset (constant 200), scrypt hashing with 32-byte salt, session invalidation on password reset, all reset tokens for email consumed after use, good security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options), CORS restricted to single origin, body size limit. Deductions: /api/metrics endpoint is completely unauthenticated — exposes active session count, locked accounts, total users, and per-endpoint request/error stats (information disclosure). Brute-force lockout is per-username only, not per-IP+username, so any attacker can lock out any account (DoS vector). Reset tokens logged to stdout. Demo credentials logged on startup. Scrypt uses default cost parameters without explicitly setting them higher for 2026 standards. No __Host- cookie prefix. Password complexity is length-only (no character class requirements). ERROR_HANDLING (6/10): Good error hierarchy (InputError, AuthError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, RateLimitError) with centralized catch block that returns appropriate HTTP status codes. Generic 500 for unexpected errors without leaking stack traces. unhandledRejection and uncaughtException handlers. readBody handles parse errors and oversize payloads. Graceful shutdown with forced timeout. Deductions: readBody has a race — after req.destroy() on oversize body, the 'end' handler can still fire and call resolve after reject. No request-level timeout means slow loris can hang promises indefinitely. json() does not check res.writableEnded before writing, risking double-write errors. Rate limiter interval timers not cleared on shutdown (only cleanupTimer is cleared). BEST_PRACTICES (6/10): Clean organization with numbered sections, JSDoc type annotations, fail-fast env validation, configurable TTLs, bounded Maps with eviction, separation of concerns, module exports for testability, 'use strict'. Deductions: In-memory stores only — no persistence, no horizontal scaling capability. O(n) linear scan to find user by email (should use index). sanitize() silently mutates usernames before validation instead of rejecting invalid input. No request logging or audit trail, which is critical for authentication systems. scryptSync for demo user vs async scrypt elsewhere (inconsistency). No API versioning. ROBUSTNESS (5/10): Bounded collections with periodic cleanup prevent unbounded growth. Multi-tier rate limiting (global 100/min, auth 10/min, reset 5/hr). Graceful shutdown with SIGTERM/SIGINT/uncaughtException handling and 10s forced exit. Deductions: No request timeout — handlers can hang indefinitely. No HTTP server connection limit. Memory-only storage is a single point of failure with zero persistence. Cleanup sorting creates O(n log n) copies of entire session map every cycle. Password reset email is only logged, never actually sent (task explicitly requires the flow). No circuit breaker or retry logic for any downstream dependency.
